      ```lean
          ⟨hZU0, hZO⟩, hΨ_diff.mono (Set.inter_subset_left), ?_, ?_⟩
        · intro G hG
          rcases hO_sub_image ⟨hG.1.2, hG.2⟩ with ⟨z, hzUvec, rfl⟩
          exact ⟨z, hUvec_sub hzUvec, rfl⟩
        · intro w hw hwUscalar
          have hwU_helper :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ∈
                (U0 ∩ O) ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
            ⟨hwUscalar, ⟨w, rfl⟩⟩
          exact BHW.hallWightman_powerSeriesChart_branch_eq_of_sameGram
            (d := d) hd n F hBranch hΨ_branch_vec hUvec_sub hO_sub_image
            hw hwU_helper
      ```

      The final helper in the last line is not a new source theorem: it is the
      branch-law transport step.  Given `sourceMinkowskiGram w ∈ O`, Lemma 3
      returns `z ∈ Uvec` with the same Gram; `hΨ_branch_vec` identifies
      `Ψ (sourceMinkowskiGram z)` with `extendF F z`, and `hBranch` identifies
      `extendF F z` with `extendF F w`.

      The exceptional theorem is the final paragraph of Hall-Wightman's proof.
      First use the same-source-Gram branch law to define the scalar germ
      `phi` on `S'_n`.  Lemmas 1--7 give its single-valuedness, continuity, and
      local boundedness.  On the complement of the exceptional determinantal
      locus, `hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at` supplies ambient
      holomorphic representatives.  Since the exceptional locus is locally an
      analytic subvariety of the source Gram variety, the removable
      singularity theorem extends the scalar representative through `Z`.
      This is the precise mathematical content of
      `hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at`.

      The compatible germ-atlas theorem must remember which local chart was
      obtained at each scalar point and must prove its `overlap_eq` field by
      the Hall-Wightman power-series/removable-germ uniqueness argument.  Do
      not replace that field by a generic predicate saying that arbitrary
      ambient holomorphic extensions agree when they agree on the
      rank-bounded Gram variety; that statement is false in general and would
      be assumption-smuggling.

      Lean-shaped proof of the chart split after these subfacts:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_localScalarChart_at ... := by
        exact BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at
          (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hZU hRank

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at ... := by
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch with
          ⟨phi, hphi_branch, hphi_cont, hphi_bdd⟩
        have hMaxCharts :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆
                  BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
          intro W hWU hWmax
          rcases BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at
              (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hWU hWmax with
            ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hWU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, hΨ_branch⟩
          refine ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hWU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, ?_⟩
          intro G hG
          have hGdomain : G ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n :=
            hU0_sub hG
          rcases hGdomain with ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩
          exact (hphi_branch w hw).trans (hΨ_branch w hw hG.1).symm
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_removableScalarChart_at
            (d := d) hd n hphi_cont hphi_bdd
            (BHW.hwSourceGramExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety d n)
            hMaxCharts hZU hRank with
          ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hZU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, hphi_eq⟩
        refine ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hZU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, ?_⟩
        intro w hw hwU0
        have hG : BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ∈
            U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
          ⟨hwU0, ⟨w, rfl⟩⟩
        exact (hphi_eq hG).symm.trans (hphi_branch w hw)
      ```

      Do not add a separate production descent-core forwarding theorem merely
      to package the local facts into the descent theorem.  The active Lean
      target is the direct
      proof of `sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent` above, where
      the branch-defined `Phi`, max-rank charts, and exceptional removable
      chart are in the same proof term.  If the implementation uses
      `HallWightmanScalarGermAtlas` internally for organization, the atlas
      construction should be inlined or kept private; it is not an additional
      public theorem surface on the route.

      This is the mathematically complete Hall-Wightman output needed by the
      corrected source representative.  Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7 supply the
      local power-series/implicit-function expansions in scalar products; the
      overlap compatibility comes from the same branch law above, and the
      singular points use the bounded/continuous extension and removable
      singularity step in the proof of Theorem I.  The proof never promotes
      equality on the scalar variety to equality on a full ambient open set.

      Once this local source predicate is available on every point of
      `closure E`, the compact common-germ theorem is a finite gluing theorem:

      The two source-supplier lemmas used by the adjacent OS45 packet are
      identity-route lemmas, not a general arbitrary-`ρ` theorem.  The
      Figure-2-4 selector below only constructs the active identity OS45
      common edge and the adjacent swap branch over that same edge.  A later
      generic common-boundary theorem may consume explicit source-germ
      hypotheses for any branch label, but the Figure-2-4 supplier itself must
      stay at the exact active surface:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_id
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hUfig_source :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hVcl : closure V ⊆ Ufig) :
          ∀ x ∈ closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)

      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hUfig_source :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hV_swap_ordered_closure :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ)
          (hV_figPath_closure :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              let y :=
                BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
              ∃ (Γ Δ : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
                Continuous Γ ∧
                Continuous Δ ∧
                Γ (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∧
                Γ (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) ∧
                (∀ t, Γ t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
                (∀ t, Δ t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
                (∀ t,
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Δ t) =
                    BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Γ t))))
          (hVcl : closure V ⊆ Ufig) :
          ∀ x ∈ closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩)
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)
      ```

      Both displayed suppliers should factor through the same one-branch
      source theorem.  This theorem is the exact OS I §4.5/BHW input needed
      upstream of EOW:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_oneBranch_sourcePatch
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ℕ) (β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (U : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hU_ordered :
            ∀ x ∈ U,
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) β)
          (hU_horiz_bhw :
            ∀ x ∈ U,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0U : x0 ∈ U)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n β
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x0)) :
          BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
            (d := d) OS lgc n β
            (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x0)
      ```

      Implementation restriction: this arbitrary-`β` theorem is only an
      assembly target because it consumes `hScalar`.  The scalar packet is the
      real mathematical source input.  The current theorem-2 route needs only
      two public scalar-packet suppliers: `β = 1` and
      `β = Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩`.  If the generic theorem is
      implemented before the full arbitrary-permutation Hall-Wightman source
      theorem, it must remain this private-style assembly lemma consuming the
      explicit scalar source packet returned by the identity or adjacent scalar
      source suppliers, not a theorem claiming that `hU_open`, `hU_ordered`,
      and `hU_horiz_bhw` alone prove arbitrary permutation source
      compatibility.  Jost and ordinary extended-tube fields live upstream in
      the scalar-packet suppliers; once `hScalar` is present, this assembly
      step does not need to carry them.

      Mathematical content of this one-branch theorem:

      1. Put `Q := os45QuarterTurnCLE` and
         `Wβ z := permAct β.symm (Q.symm z)`.
      2. Use `hU_ordered x0 hx0U` and the checked
         `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered` to put the
         horizontal base point in `os45ACRBranchDomain β`.  Use
         `hU_horiz_bhw x0 hx0U` to put the same base point in
         `os45PulledRealBranchDomain β`.  Both are open domains, so shrink to
         a chart neighborhood contained in their intersection.  On that
         neighborhood, `Q.symm z ∈ ForwardTube` and `Wβ z ∈ ExtendedTube`.
      3. On this neighborhood the BHW-side representative is definitionally
         `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z)`.
      4. The ACR-side equality

         ```lean
         extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z) =
           bvt_F OS lgc n (Wβ z)
         ```

         on `os45ACRBranchDomain β` is the genuine one-branch BHW
         source/edge theorem: the OS-II ACR branch and the Hall-Wightman BHW
         branch are two continuations of the same forward-tube germ through the
         local Streater-Wightman Figure-2-4 real environment.  It may use the
         one-branch scalar-product representative and local source uniqueness
         for the branch `β`, but it may not use adjacent branch equality,
         `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`, final `bvt_W` locality, or global
         PET branch independence.
      5. Package the branch-specific neighborhood and the two agreement fields
         as `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`.

      The identity supplier applies this theorem not to bare `Ufig`, but to
      the ordered open patch

      ```lean
      Uid := Ufig ∩
        EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
          (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
      ```

      with `β = 1` and `x0 = x`.  For a closure point
      `x ∈ closure V`, this is an open neighborhood of `x`; the required
      membership in `Uid` is obtained from `hVcl` and
      `hV_ordered_closure`.  Apply
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id` with `V := Uid`: the Jost,
      ordinary extended-tube, and identity horizontal pulled-domain hypotheses
      are the corresponding fields of `hUfig_source` restricted along
      `Uid ⊆ Ufig`.  Then apply the generic one-branch source-patch assembly
      with the same `Uid` and the resulting scalar packet.  No extra shrink is
      needed at this layer unless a later compactness consumer explicitly asks
      for one; the branch-BV compact shrink is a separate downstream step.

      For the adjacent supplier, do not try to use the original `V` as the
      scalar source patch for a boundary point `x ∈ closure V`.  First choose a
      local open patch around that closure point in the original identity
      coordinates:

      ```lean
      Ux := Ufig ∩
        EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
          (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) ∩
        {u | (fun k => u (τ k)) ∈
          EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ} ∩
        {u | BHW.realEmbed
          (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) u) ∈
          BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ}
      ```

      The point `x` lies in `Ux` by `hVcl`, `hV_ordered_closure`,
      `hV_swap_ordered_closure`, and the closure-level adjacent pulled-domain
      field supplied by the source patch selector.  Openness comes from
      `hUfig_open`, ordered-sector openness, continuity of
      `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint`/`realEmbed`, and
      `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`.  The pointwise
      `hfig_x0` input for the adjacent scalar source theorem is exactly
      `hV_figPath_closure x hx`.

      The adjacent scalar theorem is applied on `Ux` and returns a scalar packet
      at the identity common edge

      ```lean
      τ := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
      y0 := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
        (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
      hScalar_y0 :
        BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket (d := d) OS lgc n τ y0
      ```

      The one-branch source-patch assembly theorem is not applied to `Ux`
      directly, because its ordered input for branch `τ` is an ordered
      `τ`-configuration.  Apply it to the relabelled patch

      ```lean
      Uτ := {u | (fun k => u (τ k)) ∈ Ux}
      ```

      The relabelled patch transport is part of the theorem, not an informal
      convention.  This preimage formulation is the Lean-facing definition; it
      is equivalent to the image formulation
      `{u | ∃ v ∈ Ux, u = fun k => v (τ k)}` because `τ` is an adjacent swap.
      For the base point,

      ```lean
      let xτ : NPointDomain d n := fun k => x (τ k)
      have hxτUτ : xτ ∈ Uτ := by
        change (fun k => xτ (τ k)) ∈ Ux
        simpa [xτ, τ] using hxUx
      ```

      For a general `u ∈ Uτ`, put `v := fun k => u (τ k)`.  Then
      `hvUx : v ∈ Ux` is exactly `hu`, and

      ```lean
      have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
        ext k μ
        simp [v, τ]
      ```

      The transported source-patch fields are then:

      - openness of `Uτ`, by continuity of the finite permutation map
        `u ↦ fun k => u (τ k)` and the openness of `Ux`;
      - Jost membership, from the `Ufig` Jost field applied to `v` and
        `BHW.jostSet_permutation_invariant`;
      - branch-`τ` ordered-sector membership, exactly the swapped ordered field
        in the definition of `Ux`;
      - ordinary extended-tube membership of `u`, from the adjacent ET field of
        `hUfig_source` applied to `v`;
      - horizontal pulled-BHW membership, by rewriting `u` with `hu_eq` and then
        rewriting `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint τ (v ∘ τ)` to
        `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 v` using
        `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq`, then applying the
        adjacent horizontal field of `hUfig_source` to `v`.

      The Lean-facing transport transcript is:

      ```lean
      have hperm_cont :
          Continuous (fun u : NPointDomain d n => fun k => u (τ k)) := by
        refine continuous_pi ?_
        intro k
        refine continuous_pi ?_
        intro μ
        exact (continuous_apply μ).comp (continuous_apply (τ k))

      have hUτ_open : IsOpen Uτ := by
        simpa [Uτ] using hUx_open.preimage hperm_cont

      have hUτ_jost : ∀ u ∈ Uτ, u ∈ BHW.JostSet d n := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (τ k)
        have hvUx : v ∈ Ux := by simpa [Uτ, v] using hu
        have hvJ : v ∈ BHW.JostSet d n := hUx_jost v hvUx
        have hτJ : (fun k => v (τ k)) ∈ BHW.JostSet d n :=
          BHW.jostSet_permutation_invariant (d := d) (n := n) τ hvJ
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
          ext k μ
          simp [v, τ]
        simpa [hu_eq] using hτJ

      have hUτ_ordered :
          ∀ u ∈ Uτ,
            u ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (τ k)
        have hvUx : v ∈ Ux := by simpa [Uτ, v] using hu
        have hv_ordered :
            (fun k => v (τ k)) ∈
              EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ :=
          hUx_swap_ordered v hvUx
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
          ext k μ
          simp [v, τ]
        simpa [hu_eq] using hv_ordered

      have hUτ_ET :
          ∀ u ∈ Uτ, BHW.realEmbed u ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (τ k)
        have hvUx : v ∈ Ux := by simpa [Uτ, v] using hu
        have hvET :
            BHW.realEmbed (fun k => v (τ k)) ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          hUx_swap_ET v hvUx
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
          ext k μ
          simp [v, τ]
        simpa [hu_eq] using hvET

      have hUτ_horiz :
          ∀ u ∈ Uτ,
            BHW.realEmbed
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ u) ∈
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (τ k)
        have hvUx : v ∈ Ux := by simpa [Uτ, v] using hu
        have hbase := hUx_horiz v hvUx
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
          ext k μ
          simp [v, τ]
        have hedge :
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ u =
              BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) v := by
          rw [hu_eq]
          simpa [τ] using
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
              (d := d) (n := n) i hi (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) v
        simpa [hedge] using hbase
      ```

      The basepoint rewrite must be explicit.  Put

      ```lean
      have hedge_eq :
        BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ xτ = y0 := by
        simpa [τ, xτ, y0] using
          BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
            (d := d) (n := n) i hi (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x

      have hScalar_relabelled :
        BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
          (d := d) OS lgc n τ
          (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ xτ) := by
        simpa [hedge_eq] using hScalar_y0
      ```

      Applying the one-branch theorem with `β = τ`, `U = Uτ`, and `x0 = xτ`
      consumes `hScalar_relabelled` and yields the adjacent source germ at
      `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint τ xτ`.  The final displayed supplier rewrites
      this predicate back to `y0` by the same `hedge_eq`.  This is only
      basepoint transport in the source-germ predicate; it is not equality
      between the identity and adjacent branch values.

      Their proof is not an extra route, but it also must not silently promote
      domain geometry into branch-value equality.  The fields of
      `hUfig_source` say only that the selected Figure-2-4 patch is Jost, that
      the identity and adjacent real configurations lie in the ordinary
      extended tube, and that the two OS45 horizontal chart points lie in the
      corresponding pulled BHW domains.  These fields are enough to choose
      neighborhoods and to evaluate the two formulas.  They do **not** prove
      that the formulas agree.

      For the identity supplier, after `β = 1`, the branch-specific pulled BHW
      argument is definitionally `Q.symm z`.  For the adjacent supplier, the
      proof must work on the relabelled ordered configuration `x ∘ τ`, apply
      the same one-branch OS I §4.5 / BHW source construction there with the
      pointwise Figure-2-4 path supplied by `hV_figPath_closure x hx`, and
      then transport the result back to the identity common edge by
      `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq` and
      `BHW.os45QuarterTurnConfig_reindexed_realBranch_eq`.  It must not prove
      or assume equality between the identity and adjacent BHW branches.

      Lean implementation rule: `os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent`
      may call only the branch-local OS I §4.5/BHW source construction for the
      relabelled one-branch packet, plus the geometric reindexing identities.
      If a proof calls the downstream
      `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarGerm_of_OSII_Figure24`, the downstream
      `BHW.bvt_F_adjacent_extendF_edgeDistribution_eq_of_osEOWDifferenceEnvelope`,
      `hallWightman_source_permutedBranch_compatibility_of_distributionalAnchor`,
      `BHW.permutedExtendedTube_singleValued_of_forwardTube_symmetry`, or a
      corollary of them, it has skipped Slot 1 and is off the OS-II route.

      Readiness hardening for the one-branch theorem:

      The displayed `os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_oneBranch_sourcePatch`
      theorem must not be implemented as if its source-patch geometry
      hypotheses alone implied value agreement.  Those hypotheses give only:

      - a real source patch `U`;
      - ordered-sector membership, which supplies the ACR-side base point;
      - horizontal membership in the branch-specific pulled BHW domain.

      They are enough to choose neighborhoods and evaluate formulas.  They are
      not enough to identify the ACR branch and the pulled BHW branch.  They
      also do not replace the Jost and ordinary extended-tube hypotheses used
      upstream to produce the scalar packet.  The value-identification part
      must be isolated as the following genuine one-branch source theorem,
      whose value input is the scalar packet:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_sourcePatch
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (U : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hU_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ U ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) β)
          (hU_horiz_bhw :
            ∀ x, x ∈ U ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0U : x0 ∈ U)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n β
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x0)) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let Wβ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) β.symm (Q.symm z)
          ∃ Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
            IsOpen Uy ∧
            BHW.realEmbed
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x0) ∈ Uy ∧
            (∀ z, z ∈ Uy -> Wβ z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            DifferentiableOn ℂ
              (fun z =>
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z))
              Uy ∧
            (∀ z, z ∈ Uy ->
              z ∈ BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β ->
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z) =
                  BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n β z)
      ```

      This theorem is not a wrapper: its conclusion is exactly the
      branch-local ACR/BHW equality supplied by `hScalar`, together with the
      domain and holomorphy fields needed by the source germ.  After it is
      proved, `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt` is packaged mechanically by
      adding the definitional BHW-side equality on
      `os45PulledRealBranchDomain β`.

      For Lean implementation, the arbitrary-`β` theorem above must not be
      exposed as a public theorem from only `hU_open`, `hU_ordered`, and
      `hU_horiz_bhw`.  The display below is the private-style algebraic
      assembly from an already constructed scalar packet; it is not a license
      to prove arbitrary permutation source compatibility from domain geometry.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (y0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n β y0) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let Wβ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) β.symm (Q.symm z)
          ∃ Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
            IsOpen Uy ∧
            BHW.realEmbed y0 ∈ Uy ∧
            Uy ⊆ BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β ∩
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β ∧
            Set.EqOn
              (fun z => BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z))
              (fun z =>
                BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n β z)
              Uy
      ```

      The hard mathematical work is producing `hScalar` by the identity or
      adjacent OS I §4.5/BHW source theorem.  This assembly theorem then
      converts the scalar packet to the ACR/BHW equality by the algebraic
      reduction below.  The broader
      `os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_sourcePatch` theorem adds the
      source-patch domain hypotheses, records differentiability of the common
      representative using `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch_holomorphicOn`, and
      packages the same neighborhood for `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`.

      The source input itself has one algebraic reduction that must be used in
      Lean before any harder Hall-Wightman continuation is attempted.  Once a
      local scalar-product representative has supplied equality of the two
      Gram arguments, the OS45 equality is just calculation:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          (hUy_acr :
            Uy ⊆ BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β)
          (hUy_bhw :
            Uy ⊆ BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β)
          (hGram :
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ∀ z, z ∈ Uy ->
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n β.symm
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z))) =
                hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z))) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let Wβ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) β.symm (Q.symm z)
          Set.EqOn
            (fun z => BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z))
            (fun z =>
              BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n β z)
            Uy
      ```

      Proof transcript for this reduction:

      1. For `z ∈ Uy`, put `u := Q.symm z` and `w := Wβ z`.
         Unfolding `BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain`, `hUy_acr` gives
         `hu_ft : u ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n`; unfolding
         `BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain`, `hUy_bhw` gives
         `hw_et : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n`.
      2. Use `(hRep.branch_eq w hw).symm` to rewrite
         `BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w` as
         `hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)`.
      3. Use `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm` to rewrite
         `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w` as
         `BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n β.symm
           (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u)`, then use `hGram z hz` to replace
         this scalar value by
         `hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u)`.
      4. Set `hu_et := BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hu_ft`.  Use
         `hRep.branch_eq u hu_et` to rewrite the scalar value as
         `BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u`.
      5. Use `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` to rewrite
         `BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u` as `bvt_F OS lgc n u`.  The
         current production theorem consumes `bvt_F_holomorphic`, transported
         to `BHW.ForwardTube`, and
         `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`; the stronger checked
         `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` remains the input for
         the Hall-Wightman same-source-Gram branch law, not for this
         forward-tube simplification.
      6. Unfold `BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative`.  It is
         `bvt_F OS lgc n w`; the total permutation field
         `bvt_F_perm OS lgc n β.symm u` gives
         `bvt_F OS lgc n w = bvt_F OS lgc n u`, so the final equality is this
         equality in the reverse direction.

      Lean-oriented calculation:

      ```lean
      intro z hz
      let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
      let u : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ := Q.symm z
      let w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
        BHW.permAct (d := d) β.symm u
      have hu_ft : u ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
        simpa [BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain, Q, u] using hUy_acr hz
      have hw_et : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        simpa [BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain, Q, u, w] using hUy_bhw hz
      have hu_et : u ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
        BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hu_ft
      have hbranch_w :
          BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w =
            hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :=
        (hRep.branch_eq w hw_et).symm
      have hgram_w :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w =
            BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n β.symm
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u) := by
        simpa [w, BHW.permAct] using
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm (d := d) (n := n) β.symm u
      have hbranch_u :
          hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u) =
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u :=
        hRep.branch_eq u hu_et
      have hF_holo_BHW :
          DifferentiableOn ℂ (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
        simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
          bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
      have hF_restricted_BHW :
          ∀ (Λ : RestrictedLorentzGroup d)
            (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
            z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
            bvt_F OS lgc n
              (fun k μ => ∑ ν, (Λ.val.val μ ν : ℂ) * z k ν) =
            bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
        intro Λ z hz
        exact bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
          (d := d) OS lgc n Λ z
          ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) ▸ hz)
      have hext_u :
          BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u = bvt_F OS lgc n u :=
        BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
          hF_holo_BHW hF_restricted_BHW u hu_ft
      have hperm_u :
          bvt_F OS lgc n w = bvt_F OS lgc n u := by
        simpa [w, BHW.permAct] using bvt_F_perm OS lgc n β.symm u
      calc
        BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w
            = hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) := hbranch_w
        _ = hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n β.symm
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u)) := by rw [hgram_w]
        _ = hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u) := by
          simpa [Q, u] using hGram z hz
        _ = BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u := hbranch_u
        _ = bvt_F OS lgc n u := hext_u
        _ = BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n β z := by
          simpa [BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative, Q, u, w] using hperm_u.symm
      ```

      Thus the only hard part of `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput`
      is not an OS45 coordinate calculation: it is the local
      Hall-Wightman scalar equality `hGram`.  That equality must be supplied
      from the one-branch OS I §4.5 / Streater-Wightman-Hall-Wightman source
      theorem on the selected Figure-2-4 real environment.  It must not be
      replaced by the later global PET branch-independence theorem or by the
      downstream real-edge equality extracted from an
      `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`.

      Do not try to prove `hGram` from `SourceScalarRepresentativeData` alone.
      That data only says that the ordinary extended-tube branch is represented
      by `Phi` on `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`; it does not assert any
      permutation invariance of `Phi`.  The missing input is a local
      Hall-Wightman theorem for the symmetric `S'_n` datum producing the
      packet `BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket`.  There is no public
      arbitrary-`β` theorem that produces this packet from the five
      source-domain hypotheses alone.  The active Lean surfaces are:

      - `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id`, the specialization
        `β = 1`;
      - `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`, the
        specialization `β = Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩`;
      - at most a private assembly helper
        `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_of_scalarCorridor`, whose hypotheses
        already include the appropriate scalar corridor and seed equality.

      The two public active surfaces must therefore be written explicitly.
      The identity source input is the harmless normalization case:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hV_ET :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_horiz_id :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 ∈ V) :
          let β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := 1
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x0
          BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
            (d := d) OS lgc n β y0
      ```

      Its proof is only the identity simplification.  Obtain `hRep` from
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`.  Use
      `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered` and
      `hV_horiz_id x0 hx0V` to define
      `Uy` as the finite intersection of the two open branch domains and the
      preimage of `hRep.U` under
      `z ↦ sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)`.  Take
      `Wscal := hRep.U`, using `hRep.U_relOpen`.  The base point belongs to
      `Uy` because the ordered hypothesis puts `Q.symm (realEmbed y0)` in the
      forward tube, hence in the extended tube, and `hV_horiz_id` puts the
      branch-undone point in the pulled BHW domain.  Since `β = 1`,
      `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n β.symm` is reduced by the named
      helper
      `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq`; this is a `simp`
      consequence of the actual definition and
      `BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one`, not a definitional equality to be
      left implicit.  The equality field is then `rfl` after
      simp-normalizing `β` and `sourcePermuteComplexGram_one`; no
      connectedness, path, or adjacent source theorem is used in the identity
      case.

      Lean normalization used in the identity supplier:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) =
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
        ext Z
        simp [BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain,
          BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one]

      -- Inside `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id`.
      let β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := 1
      have hDouble_one :
          BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) =
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n :=
        BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq (d := d) (n := n)
      have hW_double :
          hRep.U ⊆
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n β.symm := by
        intro Z hZU
        have hZU' : Z ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
          simpa [hRep.U_eq] using hZU
        have hZdouble :
            Z ∈ BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) := by
          simpa [hDouble_one] using hZU'
        simpa [β] using hZdouble
      have hEq_id :
          Set.EqOn
            (fun Z =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n β.symm Z))
            hRep.Phi
            hRep.U := by
        intro Z hZU
        simp [β, BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one]
      ```

      The adjacent source input is the genuine OS I §4.5 source theorem.  It
      must carry the pointwise Figure-2-4 path field `hfig_x0`; without that
      field the quarter-turn scalar point is not connected to the `S'_n` seed
      inside the adjacent double scalar domain.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ)
          (hV_horiz_swap :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 ∈ V)
          (hfig_x0 :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            let y0 :=
              BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x0
            ∃ (Γ Δ : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              Continuous Γ ∧
              Continuous Δ ∧
              Γ (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) ∧
              Γ (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0) ∧
              (∀ t, Γ t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
              (∀ t, Δ t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
              (∀ t,
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Δ t) =
                  BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Γ t)))) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x0
          BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
            (d := d) OS lgc n τ y0
      ```

      Proof transcript for the adjacent surface: obtain `hRep` as in the
      identity case.  Apply
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq` with
      the same selected patch `V`, the swapped ordering field, the horizontal
      pulled-domain field, and the pointwise path field `hfig_x0`; this gives
      the `S'_n` seed
      equality and the scalar path from the seed to the OS45 quarter-turn
      scalar point.  Apply
      `BHW.os45AdjacentQuarterTurnScalarCorridor_of_figure24` to thicken that
      path inside `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n τ`, obtaining the
      connected relatively open `Wscal` and the chart neighborhood `Uy`.
      The equality of
      `Z ↦ hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ Z)` and `hRep.Phi` on all
      of `Wscal` is then the source-variety identity theorem: both functions
      are holomorphic on the double scalar domain, they agree on the nonempty
      relatively open seed `Wseed ⊆ Wscal`, and `Wscal` is connected.  This is
      the only analytic continuation step in the adjacent source supplier.
      It is not local EOW and it does not mention `bvt_W`.

      There is no route permission to prove an arbitrary-permutation public
      theorem here until the same `S'_n` seed and BHW corridor have been
      supplied for arbitrary `β`.

      Proof transcript for the two active source inputs:

      1. Obtain `hRep` from
         `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`.  Its upstream proof is
         the five-stage ordinary Hall-Wightman source descent:
         `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq`,
         `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`,
         `BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent`,
         `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw`, and
         `BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData`, specialized using
         `bvt_F_holomorphic` and
         `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` after the
         `BHW_forwardTube_eq` convention conversion.
      2. Put
         `Z0 := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
           (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0))`.
         The ordered hypothesis gives
         `Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0) ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n`; the horizontal
         BHW hypothesis gives
         `BHW.permAct β.symm (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0)) ∈
           BHW.ExtendedTube d n`.  Hence `Z0` lies in the ordinary scalar
         domain and `sourcePermuteComplexGram n β.symm Z0` lies in it as well,
         after rewriting by `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`.
      3. For `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id`, set `β = 1`.
         Use `Wscal := hRep.U`; the packet requires relative openness and
         equality on `Wscal`, not connectedness or a smaller scalar
         corridor.  The chart neighborhood `Uy` is the open intersection of
         the two branch domains with the preimage of this `Wscal` under
         `z ↦ sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)`.  Since
         `sourcePermuteComplexGram n 1 = id`,
         `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq` rewrites
         `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n 1` to the ordinary scalar
         domain, and the equality
         `hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n 1 Z) = hRep.Phi Z` is by
         `simp [BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one]`.
      4. For `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`, set
         `τ := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩` and apply
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq` to
         obtain the anchored local `S'_n` scalar seed `Wseed`, its seed
         equality, and the Figure-2-4 scalar path from the distinguished
         seed point to the OS45 quarter-turn scalar point, using the pointwise
         `hfig_x0` supplied on the theorem surface.  Then
         apply `BHW.os45AdjacentQuarterTurnScalarCorridor_of_figure24` with
         the double-domain relative-openness proof supplied by
         `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`
         from `hRep.U_relOpen` and `hRep.U_eq`.
      5. In the adjacent case, propagate equality of the two scalar
         representatives
         `Z ↦ hRep.Phi Z` and
         `Z ↦ hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ Z)` from
         `Wseed` to `Wscal` by the source-variety identity theorem.  This is
         not a real-edge equality of the two `extendF` branches, and it does
         not use `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`.
      6. Pull the relatively open scalar neighbourhood back through the
         continuous polynomial map
         `z ↦ BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)` and intersect with the
         two open branch domains.  Continuity is supplied by
         `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram` and continuity of `Q.symm`; the
         branch-domain openings are
         `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain` and
         `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`.  This produces `Uy` and the
         displayed `hGram` carrier.

      The outputs of
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id` and
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent` feed
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq`, which in turn feeds
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput`.  This ordering keeps the
      scalar source theorem explicit and prevents boundary-functional
      demotion: the final `bvt_W` distribution is not replaced by a local
      scalar functional, and no real-edge adjacent equality is used upstream.

      Lean-ready internal decomposition of
      the two active scalar source inputs:

      **(A) Quarter-turn pullback geometry.**  First choose the OS45 chart
      neighbourhood and prove that its scalar image lies in the adjacent double
      scalar domain.  This step has no function equality and no real
      uniqueness environment.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchQuarterTurnPullbackGeometry_of_sourcePatch
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (U : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hU_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ U ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) β)
          (hU_horiz_bhw :
            ∀ x, x ∈ U ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0U : x0 ∈ U) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x0
          ∃ Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              IsOpen Uy ∧
              BHW.realEmbed y0 ∈ Uy ∧
              Uy ⊆ BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β ∩
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β ∧
              (∀ z, z ∈ Uy ->
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z) ∈
                  BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n β.symm)
      ```

      For the identity supplier, the double-domain condition is the
      `β = 1` specialization of
      `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq` after rewriting
      `β.symm = 1`; it should be discharged by `simp [β,
      BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq]`.
      For the adjacent supplier, apply the same geometry to the relabelled
      ordered patch `x ∘ τ`.  The scalar packet is first produced at the
      identity common edge `y0`; before this geometry theorem is consumed by
      the one-branch source-patch assembly, rewrite the packet's basepoint to
      `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint τ (x ∘ τ)` using
      `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq`, and after packaging
      rewrite the resulting source-germ predicate back to `y0`.  The proof uses
      `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered`, the horizontal BHW
      domain field, `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain`,
      `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`,
      `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`, and
      `BHW.mem_sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_iff_exists_realizations`.

      **(B) Wick-side scalar seed and BHW corridor.**  The seed must not be
      stated as equality on `sourceRealGramComplexify n G` for a real
      Minkowski Gram environment.  At the OS45 horizontal edge,

      ```lean
      let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
      Q.symm
        (BHW.realEmbed
          (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x))
        =
      BHW.os45QuarterTurnConfig (d := d) (n := n)
        (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (β k)))
      ```

      and the scalar point
      `sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (realEmbed ...))` is generally a
      genuinely complex point of the Hall-Wightman scalar variety.  It is not
      `sourceRealGramComplexify n (sourceRealMinkowskiGram d n x)`.  Therefore
      the old schematic `hSeedPatch` formulation is rejected: real-environment
      uniqueness cannot be applied directly at the quarter-turn scalar point.

      The OS I §4.5 proof instead supplies two pieces, both before any local
      EOW real-edge equality:

      1. an `S'_n` seed open set in scalar-product coordinates, obtained from
         Euclidean symmetry and the OS-II continuation package; and
      2. a Hall-Wightman/BHW scalar corridor, inside the adjacent double scalar
         domain, connecting that `S'_n` seed to the OS45 quarter-turn scalar
         neighbourhood.

      On the active theorem-2 path this is needed only for the identity branch
      and one adjacent transposition.  Do not promote an arbitrary-`β`
      production theorem unless the same `S'_n` seed and BHW corridor have
      been proved for that `β`.

      The adjacent `S'_n` seed has two internal support packets.  Keeping
      these packets explicit prevents the old real-environment mistake from
      re-entering the proof: first prove equality in a genuine complex
      source-neighborhood, then pass to scalar-product coordinates through the
      checked source-Gram local-image infrastructure.

      The scalarization chart theorem is the precise OS I §4.5/BHW input that
      relates the OS-II Wick branch functions to the scalar representative
      `hRep.Phi` on `S'_n`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              ∀ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd τ x t ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 ∈ V)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          let Φ0 :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
          let Φτ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
          ∃ (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)),
              IsOpen V0 ∧ x0 ∈ V0 ∧ V0 ⊆ V ∧
              IsOpen Usrc ∧ IsConnected Usrc ∧ Usrc.Nonempty ∧
              (∀ x, x ∈ V0 ->
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Usrc) ∧
              (∀ x : NPointDomain d n,
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Usrc ↔ x ∈ V0) ∧
              (∀ z, z ∈ Usrc ->
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈
                  BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n τ) ∧
              DifferentiableOn ℂ Φ0 Usrc ∧
              DifferentiableOn ℂ Φτ Usrc ∧
              (∀ x, x ∈ V0 ->
                Φ0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
                  bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) ∧
              (∀ φ : SchwartzNPoint d n,
                HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
                tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ V0 ->
                  ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                      Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x
                    =
                  ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                      bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k =>
                          wickRotatePoint
                            (x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k))) *
                        φ x)
      ```

      Proof transcript: shrink the selected Jost/ordered patch around the
      specified base point `x0` to one Figure-2-4 `S'_n` source chart `V0`
      with `x0 ∈ V0`.  The chart's scalar image is contained in the adjacent
      double scalar-product domain.  The identity branch scalarization is the
      ordinary forward-tube case: it uses `hRep.branch_eq`,
      `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube`, `bvt_F_holomorphic`,
      `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`, and the ordered Wick
      membership of `x`.

      **Important adjacent-trace correction.**  The adjacent Wick trace
      `fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k))` is not a forward-tube trace under
      the Figure-2-4 hypotheses.  The local hypothesis
      `hV_swap_ordered` says
      `(fun k => x (τ k)) ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector τ`; since
      `EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector τ` means that a further `τ`
      relabelling is ordered, and `τ = τ.symm`, applying
      `wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector` to this
      hypothesis gives the identity Wick trace `wick x`, not the adjacent raw
      trace `wick (x ∘ τ)`.  Therefore the adjacent scalarization must not be
      proved by `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` at `wick (x ∘ τ)`, and the
      source chart must not export a field named `wick_tau_forwardTube`.

      The missing adjacent step is a genuine OS-II/BHW scalar-trace theorem,
      but its first usable form is **distributional**, not pointwise.  On the
      Figure-2-4 `S'_n` source chart, the Hall-Wightman source scalar
      representative evaluated at the permuted source Gram has the same
      compact Wick pairings as the OS-II Euclidean adjacent Wick trace.  This
      theorem is not a wrapper and not pointwise permutation symmetry; it is
      the source-backed BHW/Jost comparison that OS I §4.5 uses before the
      corridor and real-edge EOW stage.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          let Φτ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
          ∀ φ : SchwartzNPoint d n,
            HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
            tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0 ->
              ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                  Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x
                =
              ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                  bvt_F OS lgc n
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k))) * φ x
      ```

      The tempting pointwise theorem

      ```lean
      Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
        bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k)))
      ```

      must not be used as an input to
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`.  In that
      position it is circular: it already asserts the selected adjacent
      `S'_n` scalarization on the Wick real section, while the purpose of the
      compact-Wick-to-source step is precisely to prove the adjacent `S'_n`
      source equality.  If a pointwise version is later needed, it may only be
      derived after an independent adjacent `S'_n` source theorem supplies
      continuity and boundary identification for the raw adjacent trace; it is
      not part of the seed proof.

      Proof obligations for the distributional theorem:

      1. Use only the OS-II ACR(1) continuation datum selected in
         `bvt_F_acrOne_package`, OS Euclidean permutation symmetry on
         zero-diagonal compact tests, and the Hall-Wightman/BHW source
         representative `hRep`.  Do not invoke the global pointwise theorem
         `bvt_F_perm` as a replacement for the local source theorem.
      2. The source side is the holomorphic function
         `z ↦ hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
         (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))` on `hChart.Usrc`, with holomorphy
         supplied by
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram`
         and
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram`.
      3. The OS-II trace side is the adjacent Wick trace appearing in
         `os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector`.  The proof
         compares pairings against compact tests supported in `hChart.V0`; it
         does not require, and must not assume, independent pointwise
         continuity of the raw adjacent total function
         `x ↦ bvt_F OS lgc n (wick (x ∘ τ))`.
      4. On the source side, do **not** choose an arbitrary existential
         `Δ_x` under the integral.  The Figure-2-4 path-stability theorem must
         export the canonical rotated adjacent lift
         `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift hd τ x t :=
         BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd
           (BHW.permAct τ (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath x t))`
         with membership in `ExtendedTube` on the selected chart and the
         canonical source-Gram identity.  Then `hRep.branch_eq` rewrites
         `Φτ (wick x)` as `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
         (hChart.adjLift x 0)` pointwise on `hChart.V0`; outside `hChart.V0`
         the compact support hypothesis makes the test function vanish.  This
         is a deterministic integrand, not a nonmeasurable choice of witnesses.
      5. The remaining equality of compact pairings is the OS I §4.5
         symmetric `S'_n` boundary statement: the rotated Figure-2-4
         extended-tube trace is the BHW continuation of the same Euclidean
         adjacent compact-test germ selected by E3.  This is the local
         source-import boundary if not proved internally; it must be stated as
         a pairing theorem with the exact support hypothesis above.  It is not
         local EOW, not final `bvt_W` locality, and not global PET branch
         independence.

      Implementation-level decomposition of item 5:

      The source-side pairing theorem must be proved against the permuted
      Schwinger functional, not by evaluating the raw adjacent Wick trace
      pointwise and not by integrating an arbitrary chosen `Δ_x`.  For compact
      tests supported in the selected chart, package the test as a
      zero-diagonal Schwinger test and permute it exactly as in
      `os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector`.

      The canonical lift support is:

      ```lean
      def BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (x : NPointDomain d n)
          (t : unitInterval) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
        BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
          (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
            (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t))

      theorem BHW.continuous_os45Figure24AdjacentLift
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          Continuous
            (fun p : NPointDomain d n × unitInterval =>
              BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                (d := d) (n := n) hd τ p.1 p.2)

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (x : NPointDomain d n)
          (t : unitInterval) :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
              (BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                (d := d) (n := n) hd τ x t)
            =
          BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
              (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t))
      ```

      The last theorem is exactly the already checked calculation in
      `swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists`: apply
      `BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse`, then
      `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction`, and finish with
      `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`.  The new Lean port must expose it under
      a public name rather than reprove the same argument inside the integral.

      The canonical lift as a map is only deterministic geometry: it is an
      ordinary-extended-tube representative of the same raw adjacent Wick
      section.  The boundary theorem is the later compact-pairing statement
      for `extendF` evaluated on this lift.  The Lean port must first expose
      the Lorentz normalization theorem below; that proof uses only
      Figure-2-4 geometry and ordinary `extendF` Lorentz invariance.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_extendF_eq_permutedWick_zero
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
              =
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)))
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_extendF_eq_permutedWick_zero ... := by
        classical
        let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
        intro x hx
        rcases BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse
            (d := d) (n := n) hd with
          ⟨Λinv, hΛinv⟩
        have hET :
            hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube x hx (0 : unitInterval)
        have hact :
            BHW.complexLorentzAction Λinv
                (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
              =
            BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
          calc
            BHW.complexLorentzAction Λinv
                (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
                =
              BHW.complexLorentzAction Λinv
                (BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd τ x (0 : unitInterval)) := by
                    rw [hChart.adjLift_def x (0 : unitInterval)]
            _ =
              BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                  (d := d) (n := n) x (0 : unitInterval)) := by
                    simpa [BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift] using
                      hΛinv
                        (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                          (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                            (d := d) (n := n) x (0 : unitInterval)))
            _ =
              BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
                    simpa [BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero]
        have hLor :
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λinv
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)))
              =
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) := by
          exact
            BHW.extendF_complex_lorentz_invariant
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              (by
                simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
                  bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n)
              (by
                intro Λ z hz
                exact bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
                  (d := d) OS lgc n Λ z
                  ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) ▸ hz))
              Λinv (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) hET
        simpa [hact] using hLor.symm
      ```

      The same calculation also supplies the membership theorem needed when
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.branch_eq` is applied to the raw
      adjacent Wick section:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_permutedWick_mem_extendedTube_zero
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
            BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n
      ```

      Its proof is the `hact` part of
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_extendF_eq_permutedWick_zero`, followed by
      `BHW.complexLorentzAction_mem_extendedTube` applied to
      `hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube x hx 0`.

      The raw adjacent-Wick compact-pairing theorem is itself split into a
      genuine BHW/Jost comparison and a mechanical Euclidean rewrite.  The
      comparison is the exact OS-I §4.5 content: on the selected Figure-2-4
      chart, the ordinary BHW continuation `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)` on the
      raw adjacent Wick section has the same compact pairing as the OS-II
      ACR(1) branch `bvt_F OS lgc n` evaluated on that same complex section.
      It is compact/distributional, not pointwise.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_pairing_eq_bvt_F
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          ∀ (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
            (_hφ_comp :
              HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
            (hφ_supp :
              tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0),
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * φ x
              =
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * φ x
      ```

      ACR-status audit for the right hand side: the raw adjacent Wick section
      `BHW.permAct τ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))` is **not** presently
      known to lie in `AnalyticContinuationRegion d n 1`.  The checked theorem
      `BHW.adjacent_wick_traces_mem_acrOne i hi 1` gives the identity Wick
      trace and, after the second adjacent relabeling, the identity Wick trace
      again; it does not give raw adjacent membership.  Applying
      `BHW.wickRotate_ordered_mem_acrOne τ` would require
      `x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector τ`, whereas the Figure-2-4
      hypotheses give `x ∈ ... 1` and `x ∘ τ ∈ ... τ`.  Therefore the term
      `bvt_F OS lgc n (BHW.permAct τ (wick x))` in the comparison theorem is
      used only as a total selected function whose compact pairing is
      mechanically rewritten by the finite permutation change of variables to
      the checked identity-Wick ACR pairing for the permuted test `ψZ`.  The
      proof must not cite ACR holomorphy, ACR uniqueness, or
      `bvt_F_acrOne_package.1` at the raw adjacent point.

      The purely mechanical right-side rewrite is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_bvtF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV0_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V0 -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          ∀ (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
            (_hφ_comp :
              HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
            (hφ_supp :
              tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ V0),
            let φZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                (d := d) (n := n) V0 hV0_jost φ hφ_supp⟩
            let ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * φ x
              =
            OS.S n ψZ
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of this support theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_bvtF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
          ... := by
        classical
        let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
        intro φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
        let φZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
            (d := d) (n := n) V0 hV0_jost φ hφ_supp⟩
        let ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ
        have hψ :
            OS.S n ψZ =
              ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k)) := by
          simpa [ψZ, φZ, τ] using
            bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n ψZ
        have hchange :
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * φ x
              =
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k)) := by
          simpa [τ, BHW.permAct_wickRotatePoint, Equiv.swap_inv] using
            (integral_perm_npoint_volume
              (d := d) (n := n) τ
              (fun x =>
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k)))).symm
        exact hchange.trans hψ.symm
      ```

      Thus the hard mathematical proof content behind
      `BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_pairing_eq_bvt_F` is even more
      local than the compact comparison: it is the Figure-2-4/BHW/Jost
      branch equality between the raw adjacent ordinary-extended-tube point
      and the identity Wick ordinary-forward-tube point,

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_eq_identityWick_of_BHWJost
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) =
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))
      ```

      This theorem is the local OS-I §4.5/BHW/Jost source boundary.  It is not
      a global PET-independence theorem and must not be proved by calling the
      global consumer
      `BHW.permutedExtendedTube_singleValued_of_forwardTube_symmetry`.  Its
      proof may internally use the same Hall-Wightman scalar-product
      single-valuedness theorem, but only in the specialized Figure-2-4
      setting: raw adjacent membership in the identity sector comes from
      `BHW.os45Figure24_permutedWick_mem_extendedTube_zero`, membership in the
      adjacent sector comes from `hChart.wick_id_forwardTube` and
      `BHW.permAct_mul`/`Equiv.swap_inv`, and the equality is anchored by the
      selected OS-II compact Schwinger/Jost real-environment data.  No final
      `bvt_W` locality, no `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`, and no
      prepackaged global branch-independence corollary may be used here.

      The implementation-level source input for this theorem must be local to
      the Figure-2-4 chart, not the downstream global PET package.  The
      allowed theorem surface is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalBranchCompatibility_of_BHWJost
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
            let zraw : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
              BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))
            zraw ∈ BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n 1 ->
            zraw ∈ BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n τ ->
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (fun k => zraw k) =
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (fun k => zraw (τ k))
      ```

      This is the exact local OS-I §4.5/BHW/Jost theorem: equations (4.1),
